title: Add digital signature functionality for network configurations and security policies

requirements.text: Add a digital signature field to the SecurityPolicy type in the backend that stores a cryptographic signature string for each policy

quotes: digital signatures for network configurations or security policies

acceptanceCriteria: SecurityPolicy type includes a signature field of type Text
The signature field can be null for unsigned policies
Existing policies are not broken by the schema change

requirements.text: Create a backend function to sign a security policy that accepts a policy ID and generates a cryptographic signature based on the policy content

quotes: digital signatures for network configurations or security policies

acceptanceCriteria: Function accepts a policy ID as input
Function generates a signature from policy data (name, description, type, enabled status)
Function updates the policy with the generated signature
Function returns the signed policy or an error

requirements.text: Create a backend function to verify a policy signature that checks if the stored signature matches the current policy content

quotes: digital signatures for network configurations or security policies

acceptanceCriteria: Function accepts a policy ID as input
Function validates the signature against current policy data
Function returns true if signature is valid, false otherwise
Function handles unsigned policies gracefully

requirements.text: Add a 'Sign Policy' button to each PolicyCard component that calls the backend sign function and updates the UI to show signature status

quotes: digital signatures for network configurations or security policies

acceptanceCriteria: Each policy card displays a 'Sign Policy' button
Button is disabled if policy is already signed
Clicking the button triggers the sign mutation
UI shows loading state during signing
Success/error toast notifications are displayed
Signed status is visually indicated with an icon or badge

requirements.text: Display signature verification status on the SecurityPolicies page showing whether each policy's signature is valid, invalid, or unsigned

quotes: digital signatures for network configurations or security policies

acceptanceCriteria: Each policy card shows signature status with color-coded badge
Status options: 'Signed & Valid' (green), 'Invalid Signature' (red), 'Unsigned' (gray)
Status is automatically verified when policies are loaded
Status updates when policies are modified or signed

requirements.text: Create React Query hooks for signing policies and verifying policy signatures with proper cache invalidation

quotes: digital signatures for network configurations or security policies

acceptanceCriteria: useSignPolicy mutation hook calls the backend sign function
useVerifyPolicySignature query hook calls the backend verify function
Signing a policy invalidates the policies query cache
Hooks follow the same pattern as existing useQueries hooks

constraints: Do not modify the existing SecurityPolicy CRUD operations
Maintain backward compatibility with existing unsigned policies
Keep signature generation logic in the backend actor
nonGoals: Implementing actual cryptographic signing algorithms (use mock/simulated signatures)
Adding user-specific signing keys or certificate management
Signing network configurations (focus on security policies only for this request)
Audit trail or signature history tracking
